title: French government criticized over climate record and sluggish heatwave response
subhead: While Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu has chosen to communicate minimally to avoid appearing nervous, French President Emmanuel Macron has been irritated by the criticism and has insisted that he has done 'a great deal of work.'

France's fantasy balls: 'We identify with the lives of castle dwellers from a bygone era'
subhead: Blending historical and fantastical references, fantasy balls promise a leap through time and a touch of magic in worlds reminiscent of the 'Bridgerton' series or 'Alice in Wonderland.' These evenings are as much about dance as they are about spectacle.

title: 250 years of American independence: The retreat from multiracial democracy
subhead: 'America 250' (7/13). A century after its founding, 38 states made up the nation. Deep divisions persisted over how to treat the Black population. Slavery had been abolished, much to the dismay of the Democrats, who were gaining ground. Although the Republicans won the presidential election, their victory came at the cost of institutionalizing segregationist policies in the Southern states.

title: 'Life, Larry and the Pursuit of Unhappiness' on HBO Max: An irreverent take on key moments in US history
subhead: Produced by Michelle and Barack Obama, the limited series lets Larry David, co-creator of 'Seinfeld,' take a comedic wrecking ball to American mythology.

title: At the Four Seasons in Damascus, power shifts but the elite remain
subhead: A symbol of Bashar al-Assad's grip on Syria, the luxury hotel was the scene of lavish parties and business dealings until the fall of the dictator in December 2024. After briefly serving as the headquarters for the new leaders, it is now a networking hub for entrepreneurs seeking business opportunities in a country where everything must be rebuilt.

title: To join the 'system' or overturn it? Bardella's private life and policies raise doubts
subhead: By openly seeking to appeal to economic elites and the right, far-right leader Jordan Bardella breaks with Marine Le Pen and her father's historic 'anti-system' hostility. Some party members question his desire to pursue radical policies.

title: Musée d'Orsay faces demand for restitution of Van Gogh painting
subhead: The descendant of a German Jewish art collector persecuted by the Nazis is seeking the return of the painting 'Hôpital Saint-Paul à Saint-Rémy-de-Provence.' The Commission for the Compensation of Victims of Spoliation is expected to review the case starting in September.
